notes: Codex /fork adaptation. Codex's Source is FilesUnderRoot(~/.codex/sessions): a proc-<PID> pre-session appears at launch, hands off to a rollout-keyed UUID-1 session on the first prompt, runs one turn, then /fork EAGERLY spawns a SECOND rollout (UUID-2) carrying a fresh session_id plus the replayed pre-fork history (its session_meta records forked_from_id=UUID-1, but the codex parser skips session_meta so the daemon sees UUID-2 as an independent fresh session, not a linked child). A turn on the forked thread then runs ready→working→ready on UUID-2 while UUID-1 stays frozen. The scenario-meanings 'linked new session_id if the adapter forks' is observable here only as v2_session_birth (new_session:true) — a rollout-keyed session_id never seen before. UNLIKE claudecode's in-place rewind (same session_id, transcript truncated), codex forks to a new session_id and does NOT emit transcript_removed for the original at fork time (the old rollout lingers frozen until process_exited / readyTTL sweep), so there is deliberately NO old_session_ended phase here — asserting one would demand an event codex never produces. KNOWN CAPTURE GAP: the onboarding codex driver caches the first rollout and writes a single session.uuid, so curate-lifecycle-fixture.sh filters the fixture to UUID-1 only; the v2_* phases assert what the daemon observes live but may not be present in the single-session curated fixture, surfacing as validation drift (commit-and-flag) until the codex driver tracks /fork-spawned sessions — same gap as session-reset.

text: Pre-session row (proc-<PID>) appears in ready within 1s of codex launch
After the first prompt lands, the rollout-keyed session appears in ready — proc-<PID> hands off to a fresh UUID
First user prompt drives ready → working on the rollout session
First turn completes back to ready (same rollout UUID), leaving an earlier checkpoint to fork from

Spec: 'a linked new session_id if the adapter forks' — /fork spawns a fresh rollout UUID never seen before, restored from the rewound point; this is the strongest observable proxy for 'the conversation was rewound and forked'
Next prompt after the rewind starts cleanly from the rewound point: drives the NEW forked session ready → working (must NOT match a stale working transition on UUID-1)
The post-rewind turn completes back to ready on the new forked session — clean ready → working → ready arc
